channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Philippine investigators summon VP Duterte over her public threats against President Marcos
item.description: Philippine authorities handed a subpoena to Vice President Sara Duterte’s office, inviting her to answer investigators’ questions after she publicly threatened to have the president assassinated if she were killed in an unspecified plot herself.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Police in Bangladesh arrest Hindu leader who leads rallies for minority protection
item.description: Police in Bangladesh’s capital have arrested a prominent Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation since former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina fled the country amid a mass uprising in August.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Imran Khan supporters breach lockdown in Pakistani capital, at least six people killed in violence
item.description: Supporters of imprisoned Pakistani former premier Imran Khan have breached a lockdown in the capital Islamabad to demand his release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean man convicted for deliberately gaining weight to evade military service
item.description: A South Korean man has been sentenced to a suspended prison term for deliberately gaining weight to evade military service.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least 16 tourists missing after Egyptian yacht carrying Americans sinks in high-waves Red Sea
item.description: A tourist boat called the Sea Story sank in the Red Sea on Monday near the resort town of Marsa Alam. Sixteen people are missing, including twelve foreign nationals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHL cargo plane crashes and skids into a house in Lithuania, killing a Spanish crew member
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — A DHL cargo plane crashed on approach to an airport in Lithuania's capital and skidded into a house Monday morning, killing a Spanish crew member but not harming anyone on the ground. The cause is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: SIARHEI SATSIUK, VANESSA GERA and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carrefour's cold shoulder for South American beef sparks a backlash from Brazil
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — Supermarket giant Carrefour’s support for French farmers’ protests against a trade agreement between the European Union and the South American bloc Mercosur has sparked a strong reaction in Brazil, including a refusal to supply beef to Carrefour stores in Brazil.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egyptian officials say 16 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in high waves on the Red Sea
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A tourist yacht sank in the Red Sea on Monday after warnings of rough waters and 16 people were missing, Egyptian officials said.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man charged in bomb hoax at US Embassy in London
item.description: A man was charged in connection with last week’s bomb hoax outside the U.S. Embassy in London, authorities from the U.K. said Monday. Daniel Parmenter, 43, was charged and taken into custody Monday, after appearing at Ealing Magistrates' Court. He was arrested at his home Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Scuffles in Serbian parliament as deadly station collapse sparks anger at the government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Scuffles and fistfights broke out between ruling party and opposition lawmakers in Serbia’s parliament on Monday, weeks after a deadly rail station roof collapse that ignited tensions in the Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine showdown: President says he'll fight vice president's plot to have him killed
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Monday described a public threat by the vice president to have him killed by an assassin as a criminal plot and vowed to fight it, in a looming showdown between the country’s two top leaders.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two senators urge FIFA not to pick Saudi Arabia as 2034 host
item.description: Democrats Ron Wyden of Oregon and Dick Durbin of Illinois have told FIFA president Gianni Infantino in a letter “we urge you to seek out a host country with a record of upholding human rights.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia reportedly captures a Briton fighting for Ukraine as Russian troops advance
item.description: Russia's military captured a British national fighting with Ukrainian troops who have occupied part of Russia's Kursk region, according to reports Monday, as Moscow began daylight drone attacks on civilian areas of Ukraine and its ground forces accelerated gains along parts of the front line.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Uruguay ousts conservative government, elects leftist opposition candidate as turnout hits 90 percent
item.description: Leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi claims victory in Uruguay's run-off presidential election, defeating center-right candidate Álvaro Delgado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican to install a webcam at St. Peter's tomb as part technological outreach for Holy Year
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Roman Catholic faithful will be able to view St. Peter’s tomb remotely as part of the Vatican’s technological outreach with its far-flung flock during the upcoming Holy Year. Pope Francis will inaugurate a webcam on Dec. 2 to be installed beneath the main altar of St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia's newly elected parliament opens its first session with opposition boycotting
item.description: The newly elected Georgian parliament has opened its inaugural session as opposition lawmakers and the country’s president stayed away and thousands of protesters rallied outside under a heavy police presence.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 3 Uzbek nationals arrested in the killing of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi in the UAE
item.description: The United Arab Emirates says police have arrested three Uzbek nationals for the killing of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea holds memorial for forced laborers at Sado mines, a day after boycotting Japanese event
item.description: SADO, Japan (AP) — South Korea paid tribute to wartime Korean forced laborers at Japan’s Sado Island Gold Mines in a memorial ceremony on Monday, a day after boycotting a similar event organized by Japan, as tensions over historical atrocities continue to strain relations between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Landslide and flash floods hit Indonesia’s Sumatra island, leaving 16 dead and 6 missing
item.description: Indonesian officials say rescuers have recovered 16 bodies under tons of mud and rocks or that were swept away in flash floods that hit mountainside villages on Sumatra Island.
item.dc:creator: Binsar Bakkara, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An average of 140 women and girls were killed by a partner or relative per day in 2023, the UN says
item.description: Two U.N. agencies say an average of 140 women and girls were killed by an intimate partner or family member per day last year.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel says rabbi who went missing in the UAE was killed. The government arrests 3
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel said Sunday that the body of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi who went missing in the United Arab Emirates has been found after he was killed in what it described as a “heinous antisemitic terror incident.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: U.N. reaches agreement on $300 billion climate deal for developing countries
item.description: The United Nations reached a $300 billion climate agreement for developing countries as they grapple with the significant effects of climate change. Delegates agreed Saturday to the billion-dollar pledge after “weeks of intense negotiations,” the U.N. said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Arrest warrants for Israeli leaders amplify Democratic rupture
item.description: The International Criminal Court’s (ICC) decision this week to indict Israeli leaders on charges of war crimes in Gaza has reignited the clash among Democrats over Israel's handling of the war with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taiwan's former president says US should prioritize helping Ukraine over her country
item.description: Former Taiwanese President Tsai Ing-wen suggested that the U.S. should focus on helping Ukraine before it prioritizes Taiwan in her keynote speech during the annual summit of the Halifax International Security Forum, Politico reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Philippine vice president makes public assassination threat against country's president
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said she has contracted a hired killer to assassinate her country's president in a bizarre public threat made amid worsening political divisions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look inside the most expensive house in the world — the home of the UK’s monarch
item.description: Buckingham Palace in London is worth about $4.9 billion and has about 775 rooms, including royal and guest rooms, offices and bathrooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Israel keeping its ‘eyes open’ for Iranian attacks during Trump transition period, ambassador says
item.description: Israel's Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon speaks to Fox News Digital about the threat from Iran during the Trump transition period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil’s former President Bolsonaro and aides indicted for alleged 2022 coup attempt
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro and 36 others, including aides, were indicted by federal police on Thursday for allegedly attempting a coup to keep him in office in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US briefed Ukraine ahead of Putin's 'experimental Intermediate-range ballistic' attack
item.description: The U.S. alerted Ukraine and other allies ahead of Russia's attack that Moscow might use this "experimental" ballistic weapon to help Kyiv prepare ahead of the attack on Dnipro, a U.S. official confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US citizen among 4 dead in Laos after suspected alcohol poisoning
item.description: Four people have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos after officials said that multiple people had been sickened in town popular with backpackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
